items.title: PMF vs PDF vs CDF for discrete and continuous X
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  rv["Random variable X"]
  rv --> discrete["Discrete X"]
  rv --> continuous["Continuous X"]
  discrete --> pmf["PMF p(x) = P(X=x)"]
  discrete --> cdf_d["CDF F(x) jumps at each x"]
  continuous --> pdf["PDF f(x) density"]
  continuous --> cdf_c["CDF F(x) smooth curve"]
  pmf --> sum1["Σ p(x) = 1"]
  pdf --> int1["∫ f(x)dx = 1"]
items.caption: Discrete X is summarized by a PMF with point masses; continuous X uses a PDF where probabilities come from integrals. Both share a CDF F(x) = P(X ≤ x).

items.title: From experiment outcome to ML prediction
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  outcome["Outcome ω in Ω"]
  rvmap["X(ω) numeric value"]
  dist["Distribution PMF/PDF/CDF"]
  model["Model outputs p̂ or f̂"]
  outcome --> rvmap --> dist
  model -.->|"approximates"| dist
items.caption: A random variable maps outcomes to numbers; ML models learn parameters of the distribution (e.g. softmax PMF over labels or Gaussian PDF over regression targets).
